authority: local TaxKit documentation and decision-record changes with read-only repository inspection only
acceptedOutcome: Five evidence-backed decision records accept strict lifecycle routing and publication/deployment non-claims, defer three maintainer-owned public and release-retention choices without inventing policy, and map every consequence to HGI-202 through HGI-205.

decisions.outcome: Use strict lifecycle routing and retain existing paths; physical movement or deletion requires a separately approved retention manifest.
Preserve draft metadata without inferring publication or current-truth semantics until the product and public-doc owners decide them.
Local repository evidence cannot grant external authority or prove publication, deployment, registry, production, or public availability.
Local packed artifacts are check inputs, not governed immutable candidates, until the release maintainer chooses the candidate contract.
No full-log or proof-packet retention, redaction, location, access, or deletion policy is inferred before maintainer approval.

verification.check: canonical decision-record schema and task-map equality
verification.result: All five records validate against decision-record.schema.json; affectedTasks exactly equals the taskBlocking task IDs in every record.

verification.check: source provenance and public lifecycle inventory
verification.result: Every source digest reproduces from base commit 7f0e357; all 61 tracked public MDX files and navigation carry draft metadata, while the receipt makes no publication or availability claim.
An independent evaluator accepted all five semantic choices and blockers after identifying and verifying correction of legacy metadata on the materially revised documentation-audit index.
The docs-content validator reported zero issues after the evaluator-driven metadata correction.
Repository path/type accounting, Effect language-service, lint, format, 12 skill-policy tests, Knip, production Knip, and all 23 workspace typecheck/build tasks passed.

limitations: These local decisions do not establish public-site availability, registry publication, deployment, SSR, hydration, production, or external-consumer behavior.
HGI-202 remains blocked on public draft semantics; HGI-203 through HGI-205 remain blocked where candidate and proof-retention contracts require named maintainer decisions.
nonClaims: No public content, release artifact, evidence, provider, registry, publication, deployment, or external system was mutated.
No deferred decision is represented as accepted policy.
rollback: Revert this bounded HGI-207 decision-record slice; preserve HGI-200, HGI-201, historical evidence, public content, and the prior decision provenance.
nextTrigger: Obtain the named maintainer choices for public draft semantics, immutable candidates, and release proof/log retention before completing their blocked downstream tasks.
